---
title: "Heart Disease Risk Prediction â€” BRFSS 2015"
author: "Divyanshu Sharma (23BDS1155) & Saumyarup Guha (23BDS1128)"
date: "`r format(Sys.Date(), '%B %d, %Y')`"
output:
  html_document:
    toc: true
    toc_float: true
    toc_depth: 3
    theme: flatly
    highlight: tango
    code_folding: show
    fig_width: 10
    fig_height: 6
  pdf_document:
    toc: true
    toc_depth: '3'
subtitle: Data Mining (BCSE208L) | DA2 & DA3 Analysis
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  echo       = TRUE,
  warning    = FALSE,
  message    = FALSE,
  fig.align  = "center",
  cache      = FALSE
)
```

---
```{r}


```


# 1. Load Libraries

```{r libraries}
library(tidyverse)    
library(scales)       
library(corrplot)  
library(gridExtra)
library(ggthemes)
library(caret)      
library(ROSE)    
# Models
library(randomForest) 
library(xgboost)     
library(e1071)         
library(pROC)           
library(knitr)
library(kableExtra)
```

---

# 2. Load & Inspect Data

```{r load-data}
df_raw <- read_csv(
  "heart_disease_health_indicators_BRFSS2015.csv",
  show_col_types = FALSE
)

cat("Dimensions :", nrow(df_raw), "rows x", ncol(df_raw), "columns\n")
glimpse(df_raw)
```

```{r head-table}
kable(head(df_raw, 6),
      caption = "First 6 rows of raw dataset") %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped","hover","condensed"),
                full_width = FALSE, font_size = 12)
```

---

# 3. Exploratory Data Analysis (EDA)

## 3.1 Missing Values

```{r missing-values}
missing_summary <- df_raw %>%
  summarise(across(everything(), ~sum(is.na(.)))) %>%
  pivot_longer(everything(), names_to = "Variable", values_to = "Missing") %>%
  mutate(Pct = round(Missing / nrow(df_raw) * 100, 2)) %>%
  arrange(desc(Missing))

kable(missing_summary,
      caption = "Missing value count per variable") %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped","hover"),
                full_width = FALSE)
```

> **Result:** The BRFSS 2015 dataset is complete â€” zero missing values in all 22 columns.

## 3.2 Class Distribution (Target Variable)

```{r class-dist, fig.height=5}
class_counts <- df_raw %>%
  count(HeartDiseaseorAttack) %>%
  mutate(
    Label = ifelse(HeartDiseaseorAttack == 1, "Heart Disease (1)", "No Heart Disease (0)"),
    Pct   = n / sum(n)
  )

ggplot(class_counts, aes(x = Label, y = n, fill = Label)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.55, show.legend = FALSE) +
  geom_text(aes(label = paste0(comma(n), "\n(", percent(Pct, 0.1), ")")),
            vjust = -0.4, size = 4.5, fontface = "bold") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("#2ecc71","#e74c3c")) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = comma, expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.15))) +
  labs(title   = "Target Variable Distribution",
       subtitle = "Severe class imbalance: ~9.4 % positive cases",
       x = NULL, y = "Count") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"))
```

> **Key insight:** Only **9.4 %** of records are positive (heart disease). This severe imbalance demands resampling before modelling.

## 3.3 Numeric Variable Distributions

```{r numeric-dist, fig.height=8}
numeric_vars <- c("BMI", "Age", "MentHlth", "PhysHlth", "GenHlth", "Education", "Income")

df_raw %>%
  select(all_of(numeric_vars), HeartDiseaseorAttack) %>%
  mutate(HeartDiseaseorAttack = factor(HeartDiseaseorAttack,
                                       labels = c("No HD","HD"))) %>%
  pivot_longer(-HeartDiseaseorAttack, names_to = "Variable", values_to = "Value") %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = Value, fill = HeartDiseaseorAttack)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 25, alpha = 0.75, position = "identity") +
  facet_wrap(~Variable, scales = "free", ncol = 3) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("#3498db","#e74c3c")) +
  labs(title = "Distribution of Continuous Variables by Heart Disease Status",
       fill = "Status", x = NULL, y = "Count") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(legend.position = "top",
        plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"))
```

## 3.4 Binary Variable Prevalence

```{r binary-vars, fig.height=6}
binary_vars <- c("HighBP","HighChol","CholCheck","Smoker","Stroke",
                 "Diabetes","PhysActivity","Fruits","Veggies",
                 "HvyAlcoholConsump","AnyHealthcare","NoDocbcCost","DiffWalk","Sex")

df_raw %>%
  select(all_of(binary_vars), HeartDiseaseorAttack) %>%
  mutate(HeartDiseaseorAttack = factor(HeartDiseaseorAttack,
                                       labels = c("No HD","HD"))) %>%
  pivot_longer(-HeartDiseaseorAttack, names_to = "Variable", values_to = "Value") %>%
  group_by(Variable, HeartDiseaseorAttack) %>%
  summarise(Rate = mean(Value), .groups = "drop") %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = reorder(Variable, Rate), y = Rate, fill = HeartDiseaseorAttack)) +
  geom_col(position = "dodge", width = 0.7) +
  coord_flip() +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = percent_format(1)) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("#3498db","#e74c3c")) +
  labs(title   = "Prevalence of Binary Risk Factors by Heart Disease Status",
       x = NULL, y = "Proportion", fill = "Status") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(legend.position = "top",
        plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"))
```

## 3.5 BMI Boxplot by Heart Disease Status

```{r bmi-boxplot, fig.height=5}
df_raw %>%
  mutate(HD = factor(HeartDiseaseorAttack, labels = c("No Heart Disease","Heart Disease"))) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = HD, y = BMI, fill = HD)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.alpha = 0.2, width = 0.45) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("#3498db","#e74c3c")) +
  labs(title   = "BMI Distribution by Heart Disease Status",
       subtitle = "Heart disease patients tend to have higher BMI",
       x = NULL, y = "BMI") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(legend.position = "none",
        plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"))
```

## 3.6 Age Group vs Heart Disease Proportion

```{r age-prop, fig.height=5}
df_raw %>%
  mutate(Age    = factor(Age),
         HD_Lab = factor(HeartDiseaseorAttack, labels = c("No HD","HD"))) %>%
  count(Age, HD_Lab) %>%
  group_by(Age) %>%
  mutate(Pct = n / sum(n)) %>%
  filter(HD_Lab == "HD") %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = Age, y = Pct)) +
  geom_col(fill = "#e74c3c", width = 0.7) +
  geom_text(aes(label = percent(Pct, 1)), vjust = -0.5, size = 3.2) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = percent_format(1),
                     expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.12))) +
  labs(title   = "Heart Disease Rate by Age Group",
       subtitle = "Age codes: 1 = 18â€“24  â€¦  13 = 80+",
       x = "Age Group Code", y = "% with Heart Disease") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"))
```

## 3.7 Correlation Heatmap

```{r corr-heatmap, fig.height=8, fig.width=9}
cor_mat <- cor(df_raw, method = "spearman")

corrplot(cor_mat,
         method   = "color",
         type     = "upper",
         tl.cex   = 0.75,
         tl.col   = "black",
         addCoef.col = "black",
         number.cex  = 0.45,
         col      = colorRampPalette(c("#2980b9","white","#e74c3c"))(200),
         title    = "Spearman Correlation â€” All Variables",
         mar      = c(0,0,1,0))
```

---

# 4. Preprocessing

## 4.1 Type Conversion & Factor Encoding

```{r preprocess}
binary_cols <- c("HeartDiseaseorAttack","HighBP","HighChol","CholCheck","Smoker",
                 "Stroke","Diabetes","PhysActivity","Fruits","Veggies",
                 "HvyAlcoholConsump","AnyHealthcare","NoDocbcCost","DiffWalk","Sex")
 
df <- df_raw %>%
  mutate(across(all_of(binary_cols), factor)) %>%
  mutate(
    GenHlth   = as.integer(GenHlth),
    Age       = as.integer(Age),
    Education = as.integer(Education),
    Income    = as.integer(Income)
  )
 
cat("Target class distribution after factoring:\n")
print(table(df$HeartDiseaseorAttack))
 
```

## 4.2 Outlier Capping (BMI, MentHlth, PhysHlth)

```{r outlier-cap}
winsorise <- function(x, lo = 0.01, hi = 0.99) {
  q <- quantile(x, c(lo, hi), na.rm = TRUE)
  pmin(pmax(x, q[1]), q[2])
}
 
df <- df %>%
  mutate(
    BMI      = winsorise(BMI),
    MentHlth = winsorise(MentHlth),
    PhysHlth = winsorise(PhysHlth)
  )
 
cat("BMI range after capping:", range(df$BMI), "\n")
 
```

## 4.3 Feature Selection via Information Gain

```{r info-gain}
library(FSelector)
entropy <- function(x) {
  p <- table(x) / length(x)
  p <- p[p > 0]
  -sum(p * log2(p))
}
 
conditional_entropy <- function(feature, target) {
  vals <- unique(feature)
  n    <- length(target)
  sum(sapply(vals, function(v) {
    idx <- feature == v
    (sum(idx) / n) * entropy(target[idx])
  }))
}
 
info_gain <- function(feature, target) {
  entropy(target) - conditional_entropy(feature, target)
}
 
target_vec   <- df$HeartDiseaseorAttack
feature_cols <- setdiff(names(df), "HeartDiseaseorAttack")
 
ig_scores <- sapply(feature_cols, function(col) {
  info_gain(df[[col]], target_vec)
})
 
ig_df <- data.frame(
  Feature  = names(ig_scores),
  InfoGain = round(ig_scores, 6)
) %>% arrange(desc(InfoGain))
 
print(ig_df)
```

```{r ig-plot, fig.height=6}
ggplot(ig_df, aes(x = reorder(Feature, InfoGain), y = InfoGain, fill = InfoGain)) +
  geom_col(show.legend = FALSE) +
  coord_flip() +
  scale_fill_gradient(low = "#85c1e9", high = "#1a5276") +
  labs(title = "Feature Importance â€” Information Gain",
       x = NULL, y = "Information Gain") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"))

 
```

```{r select-features}
 
selected_features <- ig_df %>%
  filter(InfoGain > 0.003) %>%
  pull(Feature)
 
cat("\nSelected", length(selected_features), "features:\n")
print(selected_features)
 
df_model <- df %>% select(all_of(c(selected_features, "HeartDiseaseorAttack")))
 
```

## 4.4 Train / Test Split (Stratified 80 / 20)

```{r split}
set.seed(42)
train_idx <- createDataPartition(df_model$HeartDiseaseorAttack, p = 0.80, list = FALSE)
train_raw <- df_model[ train_idx, ]
test_set  <- df_model[-train_idx, ]
 
cat("Training set size:", nrow(train_raw), "\n")
cat("Test     set size:", nrow(test_set),  "\n")
cat("\nClass balance in training set:\n")
print(prop.table(table(train_raw$HeartDiseaseorAttack)))
 
```

## 4.5 Class Imbalance Handling â€” ROSE Oversampling

```{r rose-balance}
set.seed(42)
train_bal <- ROSE(HeartDiseaseorAttack ~ .,
                  data = train_raw,
                  seed = 42,
                  N    = nrow(train_raw))$data
 
cat("Class distribution AFTER ROSE:\n")
print(prop.table(table(train_bal$HeartDiseaseorAttack)))
```

---

# 5. Modelling

We train three classifiers and compare them on the held-out test set.

| Model | Rationale |
|-------|-----------|
| Logistic Regression | Interpretable baseline; works well with binary outcomes |
| Random Forest | Ensemble, handles non-linearity & feature interactions |
| XGBoost | State-of-the-art gradient boosting; best on tabular imbalanced data |

## 5.1 Cross-Validation Setup

```{r cv-control}
ctrl <- trainControl(
  method          = "cv",
  number          = 5,
  classProbs      = TRUE,
  summaryFunction = twoClassSummary,   # reports ROC / Sens / Spec
  savePredictions = "final"
)

# Re-level target to valid R variable names
levels(train_bal$HeartDiseaseorAttack) <- c("NoHD", "HD")
levels(test_set$HeartDiseaseorAttack)  <- c("NoHD", "HD")
```

## 5.2 Logistic Regression

```{r logistic, cache=TRUE}
set.seed(42)
model_lr <- train(
  HeartDiseaseorAttack ~ .,
  data      = train_bal,
  method    = "glm",
  family    = "binomial",
  trControl = ctrl,
  metric    = "ROC"
)

cat("Logistic Regression â€” 5-fold CV ROC:", round(max(model_lr$results$ROC), 4), "\n")
```

## 5.3 Random Forest

```{r rf, cache=TRUE}
set.seed(42)
bal_idx   <- createDataPartition(train_bal$HeartDiseaseorAttack, p = 0.20, list = FALSE)
train_fast <- train_bal[bal_idx, ]
 
cat("Training RF on", nrow(train_fast), "rows (stratified 20% subsample)\n")
cat("Class balance in subsample:\n")
print(prop.table(table(train_fast$HeartDiseaseorAttack)))
 
ctrl_fast <- trainControl(
  method          = "cv",
  number          = 3,  
  classProbs      = TRUE,
  summaryFunction = twoClassSummary,
  savePredictions = "final",
  allowParallel   = FALSE
)
 
model_rf <- train(
  HeartDiseaseorAttack ~ .,
  data     = train_fast,
  method   = "rf",
  trControl = ctrl_fast,
  metric   = "ROC",
  tuneGrid = expand.grid(mtry = 5),   
  ntree    = 100                      
)
 
cat("\nRandom Forest â€” CV ROC:", round(max(model_rf$results$ROC), 4), "\n")
print(model_rf$finalModel)
 
```

## 5.4 XGBoost

```{r xgb, cache=TRUE}
library(xgboost)
to_numeric_df <- function(df) {
  as.data.frame(lapply(df, function(x) as.numeric(as.character(x))))
}
train_num <- to_numeric_df(train_bal)
test_num  <- to_numeric_df(test_set)
 
train_label <- ifelse(train_num$HeartDiseaseorAttack == 1, 1L, 0L)
test_label  <- ifelse(test_num$HeartDiseaseorAttack  == 1, 1L, 0L)

if (all(is.na(train_label))) {
  train_label <- ifelse(train_bal$HeartDiseaseorAttack == "HD", 1L, 0L)
  test_label  <- ifelse(test_set$HeartDiseaseorAttack  == "HD", 1L, 0L)
}
 
train_mat <- as.matrix(train_num[, colnames(train_num) != "HeartDiseaseorAttack"])
test_mat  <- as.matrix(test_num[,  colnames(test_num)  != "HeartDiseaseorAttack"])
 
cat("Train matrix:", nrow(train_mat), "x", ncol(train_mat), "\n")
cat("Test  matrix:", nrow(test_mat),  "x", ncol(test_mat),  "\n")
cat("Label distribution (train):\n"); print(table(train_label))
cat("Label distribution (test):\n");  print(table(test_label))
 
dtrain <- xgb.DMatrix(data = train_mat, label = train_label)
dtest  <- xgb.DMatrix(data = test_mat,  label = test_label)
 
set.seed(42)
cv_result <- xgb.cv(
  data              = dtrain,
  nrounds           = 150,
  nfold             = 3,
  objective         = "binary:logistic",
  eval_metric       = "auc",
  eta               = 0.1,
  max_depth         = 5,
  subsample         = 0.8,
  colsample_bytree  = 0.8,
  early_stopping_rounds = 15,
  verbose           = 0
)
 
best_nrounds <- cv_result$best_iteration
cat("Best nrounds from CV:", best_nrounds, "\n")
cat("Best CV AUC:", round(max(cv_result$evaluation_log$test_auc_mean), 4), "\n")
 
best_nrounds <- cv_result$best_iteration
if (is.null(best_nrounds) || length(best_nrounds) == 0 || is.na(best_nrounds)) {
  best_nrounds <- which.max(cv_result$evaluation_log$test_auc_mean)
}
cat("Best nrounds:", best_nrounds, "\n")
params <- list(
  objective        = "binary:logistic",
  eval_metric      = "auc",
  eta              = 0.1,
  max_depth        = 5,
  subsample        = 0.8,
  colsample_bytree = 0.8
)
 
set.seed(42)
xgb_final <- xgb.train(
  params  = params,
  data    = dtrain,
  nrounds = best_nrounds,
  verbose = 0
)
 
cat("XGBoost training complete.\n")
xgb_probs <- predict(xgb_final, dtest)
xgb_preds <- factor(ifelse(xgb_probs > 0.5, "HD", "NoHD"), levels = c("NoHD", "HD"))
test_labels_factor <- factor(ifelse(test_label == 1, "HD", "NoHD"), levels = c("NoHD", "HD"))
 
cm_xgb  <- confusionMatrix(xgb_preds, test_labels_factor, positive = "HD")
roc_xgb <- roc(test_labels_factor, xgb_probs, levels = c("NoHD", "HD"), quiet = TRUE)
auc_xgb <- as.numeric(auc(roc_xgb))
 
cat("\n=== XGBoost Test Set Results ===\n")
cat("AUC-ROC    :", round(auc_xgb, 4), "\n")
cat("Accuracy   :", round(cm_xgb$overall["Accuracy"], 4), "\n")
cat("Recall     :", round(cm_xgb$byClass["Sensitivity"], 4), "\n")
cat("Precision  :", round(cm_xgb$byClass["Precision"], 4), "\n")
cat("F1 Score   :", round(cm_xgb$byClass["F1"], 4), "\n")
cat("Specificity:", round(cm_xgb$byClass["Specificity"], 4), "\n")
res_xgb <- list(
  name        = "XGBoost",
  cm          = cm_xgb,
  roc         = roc_xgb,
  auc         = auc_xgb,
  accuracy    = cm_xgb$overall["Accuracy"],
  precision   = cm_xgb$byClass["Precision"],
  recall      = cm_xgb$byClass["Sensitivity"],
  f1          = cm_xgb$byClass["F1"],
  specificity = cm_xgb$byClass["Specificity"]
)
 

```
```{r}
# 5.5 Naive Bayes
install.packages("naivebayes", repos = "https://cloud.r-project.org", quiet = TRUE)
library(naivebayes)
set.seed(42)
model_nb <- train(
  HeartDiseaseorAttack ~ .,
  data      = train_bal,
  method    = "naive_bayes",
  trControl = ctrl_fast,
  metric    = "ROC"
)
cat("Naive Bayes â€” CV ROC:", round(max(model_nb$results$ROC), 4), "\n")
```

```{r}
# 5.6 KNN
set.seed(42)
model_knn <- train(
  HeartDiseaseorAttack ~ .,
  data      = train_fast, # same 20% subsample used for RF
  method    = "knn",
  trControl = ctrl_fast,
  metric    = "ROC",
  tuneGrid  = expand.grid(k = c(5, 9, 15)),
  preProcess = c("center", "scale")   # KNN needs scaling
)
cat("KNN â€” Best k:", model_knn$bestTune$k, "\n")
cat("KNN â€” CV ROC:", round(max(model_knn$results$ROC), 4), "\n")
```
---


## 5.7 Hybrid Model (Naive Bayes + XGBoost)

```{r hybrid-nb-xgb, cache=TRUE}
# 5.7 Hybrid Stacked Ensemble â€” NB + XGBoost 
nb_oof <- model_nb$pred %>%
  arrange(rowIndex) %>%
  pull(HD)

xgb_oof_probs <- predict(xgb_final, dtrain)  

cat("NB  probs length:", length(nb_oof), "\n")
cat("XGB probs length:", length(xgb_oof_probs), "\n")

min_len       <- min(length(nb_oof), length(xgb_oof_probs))
nb_oof        <- nb_oof[1:min_len]
xgb_oof_probs <- xgb_oof_probs[1:min_len]
true_labels   <- train_label[1:min_len]

meta_train <- data.frame(
  nb_prob  = nb_oof,
  xgb_prob = xgb_oof_probs,
  label    = factor(ifelse(true_labels == 1, "HD", "NoHD"),
                    levels = c("NoHD", "HD"))
)

cat("Meta-train dims:", dim(meta_train), "\n")
cat("Meta-train balance:\n")
print(prop.table(table(meta_train$label)))

nb_test_probs  <- predict(model_nb, newdata = test_set, type = "prob")[, "HD"]
xgb_test_probs <- xgb_probs   # already computed as predict(xgb_final, dtest)

meta_test <- data.frame(
  nb_prob  = nb_test_probs,
  xgb_prob = xgb_test_probs
)


## Step 4: Train meta-learner ----
ctrl_meta <- trainControl(
  method          = "cv",
  number          = 3,
  classProbs      = TRUE,
  summaryFunction = twoClassSummary,
  savePredictions = "final"
)

set.seed(42)
model_hybrid <- train(
  label ~ .,
  data      = meta_train,
  method    = "glm",
  family    = "binomial",
  trControl = ctrl_meta,
  metric    = "ROC"
)

cat("\nHybrid â€” Meta-learner CV ROC:",
    round(max(model_hybrid$results$ROC), 4), "\n")

hybrid_probs <- predict(model_hybrid, newdata = meta_test, type = "prob")[, "HD"]
hybrid_preds <- factor(ifelse(hybrid_probs > 0.5, "HD", "NoHD"),
                       levels = c("NoHD", "HD"))
test_labels_factor <- factor(ifelse(test_label == 1, "HD", "NoHD"),
                             levels = c("NoHD", "HD"))

cm_hybrid  <- confusionMatrix(hybrid_preds, test_labels_factor, positive = "HD")
roc_hybrid <- roc(test_labels_factor, hybrid_probs,
                  levels = c("NoHD", "HD"), quiet = TRUE)
auc_hybrid <- as.numeric(auc(roc_hybrid))

cat("\n=== Hybrid Ensemble (NB + XGBoost) ===\n")
cat("AUC-ROC    :", round(auc_hybrid, 4), "\n")
cat("Accuracy   :", round(cm_hybrid$overall["Accuracy"], 4), "\n")
cat("Recall     :", round(cm_hybrid$byClass["Sensitivity"], 4), "\n")
cat("Precision  :", round(cm_hybrid$byClass["Precision"], 4), "\n")
cat("F1 Score   :", round(cm_hybrid$byClass["F1"], 4), "\n")
cat("Specificity:", round(cm_hybrid$byClass["Specificity"], 4), "\n")

res_hybrid <- list(
  name        = "Hybrid (NB + XGBoost)",
  cm          = cm_hybrid,
  roc         = roc_hybrid,
  auc         = auc_hybrid,
  accuracy    = cm_hybrid$overall["Accuracy"],
  precision   = cm_hybrid$byClass["Precision"],
  recall      = cm_hybrid$byClass["Sensitivity"],
  f1          = cm_hybrid$byClass["F1"],
  specificity = cm_hybrid$byClass["Specificity"]
)
```

# 6. Model Evaluation on Test Set

```{r eval-helper}

eval_model <- function(model, test_data, model_name) {
  preds    <- predict(model, newdata = test_data)
  probs    <- predict(model, newdata = test_data, type = "prob")[, "HD"]
  cm       <- confusionMatrix(preds, test_data$HeartDiseaseorAttack, positive = "HD")
  roc_obj  <- roc(test_data$HeartDiseaseorAttack, probs,
                  levels = c("NoHD","HD"), quiet = TRUE)
  auc_val  <- auc(roc_obj)
  list(
    name        = model_name,
    cm          = cm,
    roc         = roc_obj,
    auc         = as.numeric(auc_val),
    accuracy    = cm$overall["Accuracy"],
    precision   = cm$byClass["Precision"],
    recall      = cm$byClass["Sensitivity"],
    f1          = cm$byClass["F1"],
    specificity = cm$byClass["Specificity"]
  )
}

# LR and RF use caret's predict  eval_model works fine for them
res_lr <- eval_model(model_lr, test_set, "Logistic Regression")
res_rf <- eval_model(model_rf, test_set, "Random Forest")
cat("res_xgb AUC:", round(res_xgb$auc, 4), "\n")
res_nb  <- eval_model(model_nb,  test_set, "Naive Bayes")
res_knn <- eval_model(model_knn, test_set, "KNN")
```




## 6.1 Confusion Matrices

```{r conf-matrix-plot, fig.height=5, fig.width=14}
plot_cm <- function(res) {
  tbl <- as.data.frame(res$cm$table)
  ggplot(tbl, aes(x = Reference, y = Prediction, fill = Freq)) +
    geom_tile(colour = "white") +
    geom_text(aes(label = Freq), size = 7, fontface = "bold") +
    scale_fill_gradient(low = "#d6eaf8", high = "#1a5276") +
    labs(title = res$name, x = "Actual", y = "Predicted") +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
    theme(legend.position = "none",
          plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5))
}

grid.arrange(plot_cm(res_lr), plot_cm(res_rf), plot_cm(res_xgb),
             plot_cm(res_nb), plot_cm(res_knn), plot_cm(res_hybrid), ncol = 3)
```

## 6.2 Performance Metrics Comparison

```{r metrics-table}
metrics_df <- data.frame(
  Model       = c("Logistic Regression","Random Forest","XGBoost",
                "Naive Bayes","KNN","Hybrid NB+XGBoost"),
  Accuracy    = round(c(res_lr$accuracy,    res_rf$accuracy,    res_xgb$accuracy,    res_nb$accuracy,    res_knn$accuracy, res_hybrid$accuracy),    4),
  Precision   = round(c(res_lr$precision,   res_rf$precision,   res_xgb$precision,   res_nb$precision,   res_knn$precision,  res_hybrid$precision),   4),
  Recall      = round(c(res_lr$recall,      res_rf$recall,      res_xgb$recall,      res_nb$recall,      res_knn$recall,  res_hybrid$recall),      4),
  F1_Score    = round(c(res_lr$f1,          res_rf$f1,          res_xgb$f1,          res_nb$f1,          res_knn$f1,  res_hybrid$f1),          4),
  Specificity = round(c(res_lr$specificity, res_rf$specificity, res_xgb$specificity, res_nb$specificity, res_knn$specificity, res_hybrid$specificity), 4),
  AUC_ROC     = round(c(res_lr$auc,         res_rf$auc,         res_xgb$auc,         res_nb$auc,         res_knn$auc,  res_hybrid$auc),         4)
)
print(metrics_df)

kable(metrics_df,
      caption = "Test Set Performance Metrics â€” All Models") %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped","hover","condensed"),
                full_width = FALSE) %>%
  column_spec(7, bold = TRUE, color = "#1a5276") %>%   # highlight AUC column
  row_spec(which.max(metrics_df$AUC_ROC), bold = TRUE, background = "#d5f5e3")
```

## 6.3 ROC Curves

```{r roc-curves, fig.height=6}
plot(res_lr$roc,  col = "#3498db", lwd = 2, main = "ROC Curves â€” All Models")
plot(res_rf$roc,  col = "#e74c3c", lwd = 2, add = TRUE)
plot(res_xgb$roc, col = "#2ecc71", lwd = 2, add = TRUE)
abline(a = 0, b = 1, lty = 2, col = "grey60")
legend("bottomright",
       legend = paste0(c("Logistic Reg", "Random Forest", "XGBoost"),
                       "  AUC = ",
                       round(c(res_lr$auc, res_rf$auc, res_xgb$auc), 3)),
       col    = c("#3498db","#e74c3c","#2ecc71"),
       lwd    = 2,
       bty    = "n",
       cex    = 0.95)
plot(res_nb$roc,  col = "#9b59b6", lwd = 2, add = TRUE)
plot(res_knn$roc, col = "#f39c12", lwd = 2, add = TRUE)
plot(res_hybrid$roc, col = "#e67e22", lwd = 2, add = TRUE)
# and add "Hybrid NB+XGB" to the legend vectors with col "#e67e22"
# Update legend to include all 5 models
legend("bottomright",
       legend = paste0(c("Logistic Reg","Random Forest","XGBoost","Naive Bayes","KNN","Hybrid"),
                       "  AUC = ",
                       round(c(res_lr$auc, res_rf$auc, res_xgb$auc, res_nb$auc, res_knn$auc), 3)),
       col = c("#3498db","#e74c3c","#2ecc71","#9b59b6","#f39c12","#e67e22"),
       lwd = 2, bty = "n", cex = 0.85)
```

---

# 7. Feature Importance

## 7.1 Random Forest Variable Importance

```{r rf-importance, fig.height=6}
rf_imp <- varImp(model_rf)$importance %>%
  rownames_to_column("Feature") %>%
  rename(Importance = Overall) %>%
  arrange(desc(Importance))

ggplot(rf_imp, aes(x = reorder(Feature, Importance), y = Importance, fill = Importance)) +
  geom_col(show.legend = FALSE) +
  coord_flip() +
  scale_fill_gradient(low = "#85c1e9", high = "#1b4f72") +
  labs(title = "Random Forest â€” Variable Importance",
       x = NULL, y = "Mean Decrease in Gini") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"))
```

## 7.2 XGBoost Variable Importance

```{r xgb-importance, fig.height=6}
xgb_imp_raw <- xgb.importance(feature_names = colnames(train_mat), model = xgb_final)
 
xgb_imp <- as.data.frame(xgb_imp_raw) %>%
  select(Feature, Gain) %>%
  rename(Importance = Gain) %>%
  arrange(desc(Importance))
 
ggplot(xgb_imp, aes(x = reorder(Feature, Importance), y = Importance, fill = Importance)) +
  geom_col(show.legend = FALSE) +
  coord_flip() +
  scale_fill_gradient(low = "#a9dfbf", high = "#1e8449") +
  labs(title = "XGBoost â€” Variable Importance (Gain)", x = NULL, y = "Gain") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"))
```

---

# 8. Best Model Summary & Clinical Interpretation

```{r best-model-summary}
best_idx <- which.max(metrics_df$AUC_ROC)
cat("=== Best Model:", metrics_df$Model[best_idx], "===\n\n")
cat("AUC-ROC    :", metrics_df$AUC_ROC[best_idx], "\n")
cat("Accuracy   :", metrics_df$Accuracy[best_idx], "\n")
cat("Recall     :", metrics_df$Recall[best_idx],
    " (sensitivity â€” fraction of true heart disease cases caught)\n")
cat("Precision  :", metrics_df$Precision[best_idx], "\n")
cat("F1 Score   :", metrics_df$F1_Score[best_idx], "\n")
cat("Specificity:", metrics_df$Specificity[best_idx], "\n")
```

**Interpretation of key risk factors (from both RF and XGBoost importance rankings):**

| Rank | Feature | Clinical Significance |
|------|---------|----------------------|
| 1 | `GenHlth` | Self-rated general health is the single strongest predictor â€” poor health consistently correlates with CVD |
| 2 | `Age` | Heart disease risk rises sharply after age group 7 (50â€“54 years) |
| 3 | `HighBP` | Hypertension is a leading modifiable CVD risk factor |
| 4 | `DiffWalk` | Difficulty walking signals underlying cardiovascular or metabolic compromise |
| 5 | `Diabetes` | Diabetic patients face 2â€“4Ă— higher CVD risk |
| 6 | `BMI` | Overweight and obesity amplify multiple metabolic risk pathways |
| 7 | `HighChol` | High cholesterol directly contributes to atherosclerosis |
| 8 | `PhysHlth` | Days of poor physical health reflect cumulative disease burden |

---
